amendments.normalized_body: Add a test in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs` that verifies when the backend binary truly does not exist (i.e., is not on PATH), `invoke()` returns `FailureClass::BinaryNotFound`. Currently the exit-code-127 test covers post-spawn classification, and retry_policy tests verify BinaryNotFound is terminal, but the actual spawn-failure → BinaryNotFound path in `process_backend.rs:986-987` (`ErrorKind::NotFound` branch) has no direct test. This is the critical non-retryable path that prevents wasting 5 retry attempts on a missing binary.

Map adapter-enforced process timeouts back to `AppError::InvocationTimeout` before they leave the service. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1037-1051` now returns `InvocationFailed { failure_class: Timeout }`, and `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:211-213` plus `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:369-373` pass that through unchanged. Existing consumers still branch on the timeout variant (for example `src/adapters/worktree.rs:892-900`), so a real CLI hang will now be treated as a generic invocation failure instead of following timeout-specific recovery/classification paths.

Make fatal availability/configuration failures terminal instead of retryable. The service always runs `check_availability()` first (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:166-169`); `ProcessBackendAdapter::check_availability()` reports missing binaries and missing `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` as `BackendUnavailable` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1171-1193`); and `AppError::failure_class()` still maps `BackendUnavailable` to retryable `TransportFailure` (`src/shared/error.rs:300-305`). After the retry-budget increase in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:45-57`, those fatal setup problems will now burn five attempts plus backoff instead of failing immediately.

Do not classify every non-zero child exit as retryable transport noise. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1442-1450` now hard-codes all exits, including `127`, to `TransportFailure`, and `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs:2038-2066` locks that behavior in. That means wrapper CLIs that start successfully but then fail with exit 127 because an interpreter or subordinate executable is missing will still consume the full 5-attempt transport budget from `src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:45-57`.

final_accepted_amendments.normalized_body: Add a test in `tests/unit/process_backend_test.rs` that verifies when the backend binary truly does not exist (i.e., is not on PATH), `invoke()` returns `FailureClass::BinaryNotFound`. Currently the exit-code-127 test covers post-spawn classification, and retry_policy tests verify BinaryNotFound is terminal, but the actual spawn-failure → BinaryNotFound path in `process_backend.rs:986-987` (`ErrorKind::NotFound` branch) has no direct test. This is the critical non-retryable path that prevents wasting 5 retry attempts on a missing binary.

Map adapter-enforced process timeouts back to `AppError::InvocationTimeout` before they leave the service. `src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1037-1051` now returns `InvocationFailed { failure_class: Timeout }`, and `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:211-213` plus `src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:369-373` pass that through unchanged. Existing consumers still branch on the timeout variant (for example `src/adapters/worktree.rs:892-900`), so a real CLI hang will now be treated as a generic invocation failure instead of following timeout-specific recovery/classification paths.

Make fatal availability/configuration failures terminal instead of retryable. The service always runs `check_availability()` first (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:166-169`); `ProcessBackendAdapter::check_availability()` reports missing binaries and missing `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` as `BackendUnavailable` (`src/adapters/process_backend.rs:1171-1193`); and `AppError::failure_class()` still maps `BackendUnavailable` to retryable `TransportFailure` (`src/shared/error.rs:300-305`). After the retry-budget increase in `src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:45-57`, those fatal setup problems will now burn five attempts plus backoff instead of failing immediately.

payload.summary: Final review accepted 3 amendment(s); restart required.
